operating_state: Total revenue increased 20% to $5.3 billion driven by Telxius and CoreSite acquisitions, with Europe property revenue surging from acquisitions and Data Centers revenue growing substantially. U.S. & Canada property revenue remained flat as colocations and amendments were offset by elevated T-Mobile churn from legacy Sprint lease cancellations. International segments delivered broad growth across Latin America, Africa, and Asia-Pacific despite foreign currency headwinds.
cost_structure: Property gross margin expanded 15% to $3.6 billion with margin improvement across international segments despite higher fuel and pass-through costs. Operating costs increased substantially driven by expanded operations and intangible asset amortization from recent acquisitions. SG&A increased driven by personnel costs supporting business expansion and CoreSite-related equity awards. The company maintains variable rate debt exposure with 10% interest rate increases resulting in additional expense.
material_events: Completed $2.3 billion common stock offering issuing 9.2 million shares at $256 per share to repay existing term loan debt. Issued senior notes using proceeds to repay CoreSite debt and maturing notes. Announced $2.5 billion Stonepeak partnership for 23% stake in U.S. data center business expected to close in Q3 2022. Acquired fiber assets in Spain and completed various site acquisitions.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation resulted in negative impact to comprehensive income with significant exposure from international revenues and operating expenses denominated in foreign currencies. The company holds EUR-denominated debt with potential losses from adverse exchange rate movements. Elevated fuel costs drove increases in pass-through revenue and expenses across international markets.
forward_outlook: Management expects substantial non-cancellable customer lease revenue from existing contracts with capital expenditure guidance including construction of new communications sites globally. Stonepeak data center partnership provides proceeds while maintaining controlling interest. The company maintains focus on international expansion and data center growth following recent acquisitions.
uncertainty_profile: T-Mobile master lease agreement terms will continue driving elevated churn rates in U.S. operations for multiple years as legacy Sprint consolidation persists. Unrecognized tax benefits could impact effective tax rate with potential changes over next twelve months, while tax assessments including amounts in India remain under proceedings. Purchase price allocations for major acquisitions remain preliminary with potential post-closing adjustments.
